lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਲਗੁ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਰਹੀਐ ਨਾਨਕ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੁਣੀਐ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਹੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jab lag duneea raheeai naanak kichh suneeai kichh kaheeai |
lines.english: As long as we are in this world, O Nanak, we should listen, and speak of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਲਿ ਰਹੇ ਹਮ ਰਹਣੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ਜੀਵਤਿਆ ਮਰਿ ਰਹੀਐ ॥੫॥੨॥
lines.translit: bhaal rahe ham rehan na paaeaa jeevatiaa mar raheeai |5|2|
lines.english: I have searched, but I have found no way to remain here; so, remain dead while yet alive. ||5||2||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Dhanaasaree, First Mehl, Second House:
The True Lord creates and adorns.
My heart is burning, and my soul is crying out in pain.
How can I remember the Lord in meditation? I cannot meditate on Him in remembrance.
By clever tricks and commands, He cannot be found.
Forgetting Him, how can one be good? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਚਿ ਮਿਲਉ ਮੇਰੀ ਮਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kiau kar saach milau meree maae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: How am I to meet my True Lord, O my mother? ||1||Pause||
How rare is the one who goes out, and searches for the merchandise of the Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕੋ ਚਾਖੈ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naa ko chaakhai naa ko khaae |
lines.english: No one tastes it, and no one eats it.
Honor is not obtained by trying to please other people.
Without You, I have no other place of rest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਪਤਿ ਰਹੈ ਰਾਖੈ ਜਾ ਸੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: taa pat rahai raakhai jaa soe |2|
lines.english: One's honor is preserved, only if the Lord preserves it. ||2||
Wherever I look, there I see Him, pervading and permeating.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਕੋ ਕਰੇ ਕੀਤੈ ਕਿਆ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: je ko kare keetai kiaa hoe |
lines.english: He may try, but what can anyone do by his own doing?

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਣਿ ਉਠਿ ਚਲਣਾ ਮੁਹਤਿ ਕਿ ਤਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: hun utth chalanaa muhat ki taal |
lines.english: Now, I shall have to get up and depart, in an instant, in the clapping of hands.
He alone is blessed, whom the True Lord forgives. ||3||
As is the Lord's Glance of Grace, so it is.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਮੁਹੁ ਦੇਸਾ ਗੁਣ ਨਹੀ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa muhu desaa gun nahee naal |
lines.english: What face will I show the Lord? I have no virtue at all.
Without His Glance of Grace, O Nanak, no one is blessed. ||4||1||3||
If the Lord bestows His Glance of Grace, then one remembers Him in meditation.
Dhanaasaree, First Mehl:
The soul is softened, and he remains absorbed in the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਿਉ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਕਾਲੁ ਨ ਖਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har siau chit laagai fir kaal na khaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: One's consciousness is attached to the Lord, and so Death does not devour him. ||1||Pause||
The duality of the inner mind is overcome. ||1||
By Guru's Grace, God is found.
His soul and the Supreme Soul become one.
Remembering the True Lord in meditation, one is enlightened.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਤੇ ਬਿਖਿਆ ਮਹਿ ਰਹੈ ਉਦਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: taa te bikhiaa meh rahai udaas |
lines.english: Then, in the midst of Maya, he remains detached.
Such is the service which the Lord's servant performs,
in the midst of children and spouses, they attain emancipation. ||2||
Such is the Glory of the True Guru;
Such a servant obtains honor in the Court of the Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਕਾ ਜੀਉ ਤਿਸੁ ਆਗੈ ਧਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis kaa jeeo tis aagai dharai |
lines.english: that he dedicates his soul to the Lord, to whom it belongs.
One who is pleasing to the Lord and Master is acceptable.
He enshrines the image of the True Guru in his heart.
He obtains the rewards which he desires.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਜਮ ਤੇ ਕੈਸਾ ਡਰੈ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: so sevak jam te kaisaa ddarai |4|
lines.english: how can such a servant be afraid of death? ||4||
The True Lord and Master grants His Grace;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪੁ ਤਪੁ ਸਭੁ ਇਹੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਹੈ ਸਾਰੁ ॥੫॥੨॥੪॥
lines.translit: jap tap sabh ihu sabad hai saar |5|2|4|
lines.english: This Shabad is the most excellent of all chanting and austere meditations. ||5||2||4||
Prays Nanak, practice contemplation,
Then, you shall find the Gate of Salvation.
and enshrine love for the True Word of His Bani.
My soul burns, over and over again.
Burning and burning, it is ruined, and it falls into evil.
Dhanaasaree, First Mehl:
cries out in pain, like a chronic patient. ||1||
That body, which forgets the Word of the Guru's Bani,
To speak too much and babble is useless.
He gave us our tongue to speak so fluently.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਬੋਲੇ ਜਾਣੈ ਸਭੁ ਸੋਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: vin bole jaanai sabh soe |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Even without our speaking, He knows everything. ||1||Pause||
He created our ears, eyes and nose.
